grades.expectation: Response mentions regularisation as an option (retrospective local authority approval)
grades.reason: Clearly describes regularisation as retrospective building regulations approval with detailed process

grades.expectation: Response mentions indemnity insurance as an alternative
grades.reason: Explicitly presents indemnity insurance as Option B alternative to regularisation

grades.expectation: Response states indemnity insurance must be obtained BEFORE any approach to the local authority
grades.reason: Prominently emphasizes this rule with warning section and states making enquiry makes indemnity unavailable

grades.expectation: Response mentions Part B (fire safety) as a key concern for loft conversions
grades.reason: Specifically identifies Part B fire safety concerns including fire doors, escape routes, and smoke alarms

grades.expectation: Response provides approximate costs for at least one option (regularisation £400-800 or insurance £50-200)
grades.reason: Provides costs for both: regularisation £400-800 and indemnity insurance £100-200
